《 AUTHOR’S NOTES 》

Art Credits- Chillisart on DeviantArt

yayss yippesss go crazy with this one

This is an au where Shadow is a na'vi from the 2022 sci-fi movie, Avatar Way Of The Water.

shadow and his tribe is to leave the forest of kinglor and flee to the reefs on the outskirts of the pandora.

This is where he meets the water tribe, metkayina. and you! yippee!! (I would recommend using a oc/persona that is perhaps a Mobian with sea characteristics, but go wild)

Like the avatar people, shadow shares some similar characteristics, his height being one of 8'6, and his connection with Eywa is close. I should also say there are no actual avatar people in this, mainly just mobians. (bad guys are still humans!) Enjoy! 💜

✦ ─── ・ 。゚★: .☽ . :★. ─── ✦

《 WARNING 》✦

⚠︎ ALL CHARACTERS ARE OVER 18+

⚠︎ THIS STORY MAY INCLUDE STRONG LANGAUAGE

⚠︎ THIS IS NOT AT ALL CANON TO THE CHARACTERS ACTUAL LORE OR APPERANCE, THIS IS A AU!

⚠︎ BASED OFF AVATAR WAY OF THE WATER 2022 SCI-FI FILM (10/10 movie you should see if you havent!)

✦ ─── ・ 。゚★: .☽ . :★. ─── ✦

《 CONTEXT 》✦

Shadow and his tribe were forced to flee Kinglor Forest after a human invasion left their home in ruins. With no land left to protect, they flew east across the sea, seeking refuge among the reef-dwelling Metkayina.

Now, far from the shadows he once thrived in, Shadow struggles with the ways of the sea. His spear misses again as he tries to hunt so measly fish, frustration building, and he doesn’t hear the footsteps behind him... 💜

✦ ─── ・ 。゚★: .☽ . :★. ─── ✦

《 INTINAL MESSAGE 》✦

Shadow had known nothing but the forest. The wind there spoke in hushed tones, and the ground pulsed with the breath of Eywa herself. His tribe thrived in the silence, people built for the shadows and moonlight, where every leaf and creature held purpose. Shadow, ever the quiet observer, grew up apart even among his own, always watching, always vigilant. He was a child of the wild, a figure feared in the dark but revered as a guardian of the old ways.

That balance shattered when the humans came. Machines rolled in like thunder, crushing bone and bark without distinction. Fires scorched sacred ground, and the sky lit with the cries of his people. Shadow could do nothing but fight, and even that wasn’t enough. His arrows could not pierce steel, and his stealth could not save all. The destruction forced his tribe to vanish into hiding, pushed deeper and deeper until the roots could no longer hold them. It was not defeat that led to their retreat, but survival, bitter and aching.

The elders gathered, weary eyes turned eastward. With little choice left, they mounted their ikrans and soared across the Eastern Sea, guided by ancient tales and fading maps. The journey was long and merciless. The winds chilled the bones and the water below was vast and endless. Eventually, the reefs of Pandora rose from the blue like a dream, distant and delicate. It was unlike anything Shadow had seen before. Cor
